default.organic: Ensure the plant receives balanced watering and compost. Prune affected leaves using clean tools.
default.chemical: Apply a mild broad-spectrum fungicide or copper spray if symptoms persist.
default.prevention: Maintain proper air circulation, avoid watering leaves directly, and keep the soil clean.

apple___apple_scab.organic: Rake and destroy fallen leaves in autumn. Apply sulfur sprays early in the season.
apple___apple_scab.chemical: Apply preventative fungicides containing captan or myclobutanil starting at green tip stage.
apple___apple_scab.prevention: Plant resistant cultivars. Prune trees to open up the canopy for better air circulation and sunlight.

apple___black_rot.organic: Prune out dead wood, cankers, and mummified fruit. Burn or dispose of infected materials.
apple___black_rot.chemical: Use fungicides containing captan or sulfur during the early growing season.
apple___black_rot.prevention: Prune trees in winter, clean tools between cuts with rubbing alcohol, and keep the orchard floor clean.

apple___cedar_apple_rust.organic: Remove nearby juniper bushes (alternate host). Apply copper or sulfur fungicides.
apple___cedar_apple_rust.chemical: Apply fungicides containing myclobutanil or triadimefon during bud break.
apple___cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Grow rust-resistant apple varieties and avoid planting apples close to cedar/juniper trees.

apple___healthy.organic: Apply organic compost annually and maintain regular pruning in late winter.
apple___healthy.chemical: No chemical treatment needed for healthy plants.
apple___healthy.prevention: Continue regular watering, monitoring for pests, and maintaining balanced soil nutrition.

potato___early_blight.organic: Apply copper fungicides or neem oil. Remove and destroy infected lower leaves immediately.
potato___early_blight.chemical: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil, mancozeb, or azoxystrobin at first sign of disease.
potato___early_blight.prevention: Practice crop rotation. Avoid overhead irrigation and water early in the day so leaves dry quickly.

potato___late_blight.organic: Remove and destroy all infected plants. Apply copper octanoate organic spray to healthy neighbors.
potato___late_blight.chemical: Use heavy-duty fungicides containing chlorothalonil, mancozeb, or copper hydroxide.
potato___late_blight.prevention: Plant certified disease-free seed tubers. Keep potato foliage dry and avoid planting near tomatoes.

potato___healthy.organic: Keep soil well-aerated. Add organic mulch to conserve moisture.
potato___healthy.prevention: Rotate potato crops every 3 years and avoid planting in fields previously occupied by tomatoes or eggplants.

tomato___bacterial_spot.organic: Apply copper-based sprays. Remove infected plants and debris immediately.
tomato___bacterial_spot.chemical: Apply copper hydroxide mixed with mancozeb to protect remaining healthy foliage.
tomato___bacterial_spot.prevention: Buy certified disease-free seeds. Avoid working in the garden when the plants are wet.

tomato___early_blight.organic: Mulch the soil base. Apply organic copper sprays. Remove infected lower leaves.
tomato___early_blight.chemical: Spray fungicides containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb as soon as spots appear.
tomato___early_blight.prevention: Provide adequate spacing. Practice a 3-year crop rotation without nightshade crops.

tomato___late_blight.organic: Uproot and burn infected plants. Protect healthy crops with copper-based organic sprays.
tomato___late_blight.chemical: Apply systemic fungicides containing chlorothalonil or copper hydroxide preventatively.
tomato___late_blight.prevention: Avoid overhead watering. Maintain proper spacing to reduce humidity around the canopy.

tomato___leaf_mold.organic: Prune the lower branches to increase airflow. Use organic sulfur sprays.
tomato___leaf_mold.chemical: Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or copper soap.
tomato___leaf_mold.prevention: Keep greenhouse humidity below 85%. Use drip irrigation to keep foliage completely dry.

tomato___healthy.organic: Maintain steady watering. Apply organic compost at the base.
tomato___healthy.prevention: Ensure good soil drainage, weed regularly, and keep a clean garden environment.

medicinal_aloe_vera___healthy.organic: Provide plenty of indirect sunlight and water only when the top 2 inches of soil are dry.
medicinal_aloe_vera___healthy.prevention: Ensure the pot has excellent drainage holes to avoid root rot.

medicinal_aloe_vera___diseased.organic: Repot in fresh succulent mix, cut off mushy infected roots, and let the plant dry out.
medicinal_aloe_vera___diseased.chemical: Apply a mild copper fungicide if leaf spots spread aggressively.
medicinal_aloe_vera___diseased.prevention: Do not overwater. Aloe Vera is a desert plant and is highly susceptible to fungal rot in damp soil.

medicinal_aloe_vera___dried.organic: Give the plant a thorough watering, move it out of harsh direct midday sun, and trim dry tips.
medicinal_aloe_vera___dried.prevention: Establish a consistent, moderate watering routine when the soil dries out fully.

medicinal_aloe_vera___chlorotic.organic: Apply balanced organic nitrogen fertilizer. Check for root binding and repot if needed.
medicinal_aloe_vera___chlorotic.chemical: Apply a chelated iron spray or balanced NPK solution if nutrient deficiency is severe.
medicinal_aloe_vera___chlorotic.prevention: Use nutrient-rich soil and ensure the plant gets enough indirect sunlight.

medicinal_neem___healthy.organic: Keep soil moderately moist. Add organic compost once a year.
medicinal_neem___healthy.prevention: Protect young neem saplings from harsh frost in winter.

medicinal_neem___diseased.organic: Prune infected leaves. Spray the tree with diluted baking soda and organic soap mixture.
medicinal_neem___diseased.chemical: Apply systemic fungicides if leaf spot or rust disease is severe.
medicinal_neem___diseased.prevention: Ensure the neem tree is planted in a sunny area with well-draining soil.

medicinal_neem___dried.organic: Increase watering frequency. Check for root infestation or trunk damage.
medicinal_neem___dried.prevention: Mulch the base of the neem tree to retain moisture in extreme summer heat.

medicinal_neem___chlorotic.organic: Feed with organic nitrogen-rich fertilizer (like blood meal) and correct soil pH.
medicinal_neem___chlorotic.chemical: Apply micronutrient sprays containing iron, magnesium, and zinc.
medicinal_neem___chlorotic.prevention: Avoid waterlogging the roots, which prevents nutrient absorption.

generic___healthy.organic: Maintain regular watering and compost application.
generic___healthy.prevention: Continue standard crop care and monitor for early signs of pests.

generic___powdery.organic: Spray with a mixture of baking soda, water, and non-detergent organic soap. Prune infected leaves.
generic___powdery.chemical: Apply fungicides containing sulfur, myclobutanil, or triadimefon.
generic___powdery.prevention: Space plants out for maximum air circulation. Avoid high nitrogen fertilizers which cause lush, susceptible growth.

generic___rust.organic: Remove and destroy infected leaves immediately. Apply organic copper or sulfur dust.
generic___rust.chemical: Apply protective fungicides containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb at the first sign of rust.
generic___rust.prevention: Water plants at the base to keep foliage dry. Clean up all crop debris at the end of the season.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.organic: Apply well-rotted compost. Keep soil consistently moist but not waterlogged.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.prevention: Grow in partial shade. Avoid dry soil conditions.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.organic: Spray with neem oil solution or release beneficial insects like ladybugs.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.chemical: Apply mild systemic insecticides if the infestation is severe.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.prevention: Inspect the undersides of leaves weekly and manually remove pests.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.organic: Prune infected leaves with sanitized tools and apply a baking soda spray.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.chemical: Use a mild copper-based fungicide if leaf spots begin to spread.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ce.prevention: Ensure good air circulation and avoid watering the leaves directly.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.organic: Apply organic potassium-rich fertilizer. Ensure it receives 6+ hours of sunlight daily.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.prevention: Mulch the soil base to retain moisture and keep the root system cool.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.organic: Amend soil with compost and apply an organic iron-rich foliar spray.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.chemical: Use chelated iron or a complete micronutrient fertilizer to correct pH/deficiency.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.prevention: Maintain slightly acidic soil (pH 6.0-6.5) and avoid overwatering.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.organic: Prune infected branches. Spray with copper soap or sulfur-based organic fungicide.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.chemical: Use systemic fungicides containing chlorothalonil or myclobutanil.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___hi.prevention: Avoid overhead watering; clear all fallen leaf debris around the base.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.organic: Water only when the soil is completely dry. Provide bright, indirect light.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.prevention: Use sandy, fast-draining cactus soil and a pot with drainage holes.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.organic: Feed with dilute organic nitrogen-rich fertilizer. Check for root binding.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.chemical: Apply a balanced water-soluble succulent fertilizer.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.prevention: Avoid compact soil and ensure the plant gets enough indirect sunlight.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.organic: Immediately stop watering. Trim decayed leaves or mushy roots and repot in fresh dry soil.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.chemical: Apply a systemic copper fungicide if leaf rot spreads.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___ka.prevention: Keep leaves dry and never let the pot sit in standing water.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.organic: Keep the soil moist and provide a trellis for climbing. Prune to shape.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.prevention: Maintain consistent watering and check for early signs of fungal spots.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.organic: Prune infected leaves and apply neem oil or baking soda solution.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.chemical: Spray with broad-spectrum organic copper fungicide.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.prevention: Ensure the vine does not get too dense; prune inner foliage for airflow.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.organic: Remove distorted growth. Spray with insecticidal soap in case distortion is caused by mites/aphids.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.chemical: Apply miticides or systemic insecticides if pest-induced distortion persists.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___mi.prevention: Keep the plant healthy and free of pest stress by regular pruning.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.organic: Provide support for climbing. Apply organic nitrogen compost monthly. Grow in partial shade.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.prevention: Avoid waterlogging and protect the plant from hot, dry wind.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.organic: Apply organic fish emulsion or compost tea. Ensure the soil is well-drained.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.chemical: Apply a mild nitrogen-rich liquid fertilizer.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.prevention: Check for root binding and ensure it is not exposed to harsh, direct sunlight.

medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.organic: Remove infected leaves immediately. Spray with a mild baking soda solution or copper soap.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.chemical: Apply a preventative copper-based fungicide at the first sign of leaf spots.
medicinal_background_remove_dataset___pi.prevention: Keep foliage dry. Ensure excellent soil drainage and ventilation around the vine.
